idea-plugin.name: Embedded Terminal Emulator
idea-plugin.description:
Embedded Terminal Emulator

Embed a professional, fast, and fully integrated terminal experience inside PyCharm without resource overhead.

Key Features and Advantages over the Official JetBrains Terminal:

  • Ultra-Lightweight Swing Native Architecture: Unlike the official terminal which uses a heavy web engine or complex HTML/JS layers that significantly increase RAM and CPU consumption, this plugin uses a pure Swing engine written in Kotlin with a hardware-accelerated glyph cache (image blitting), reducing memory consumption by 98% without additional processes.
  • Guaranteed Automatic Virtual Environment Activation: Instant detection of Python environments (`.venv`, `venv`, `env`) linked to the project and injection into the PTY process's `PATH` when opening a tab. Avoids the usual problems and misconfigurations of the official terminal to activate environments transparently.
  • Full Shell Keyboard Shortcut Control (Ctrl+R, Ctrl+C, etc.): Priority event interception that prevents the IDE from capturing critical shell shortcuts (such as history search with `Ctrl+R`, suspensions with `Ctrl+Z`, interrupts with `Ctrl+C`, or word navigation with `Opt/Ctrl + Arrows`), while maintaining accessibility of global ToolWindow shortcuts (`Cmd/Alt + 1..9`).
  • Protected Paste Mode (Bracketed Paste Mode - ANSI 2004): Native support to prevent visual corruption, long line misalignment, and unintended execution of commands when pasting multiline text into the prompt.
  • Customizable Aesthetics: Allows showing or hiding the vertical scrollbar from the plugin settings, adapting to minimalist or traditional interfaces.
  • Hot Font and Color Sync: Real-time adaptation to code ligatures, console font sizes, and PyCharm theme palettes instantly and without restarting open tabs.
  • Multiple Tabs and Lazy Loading: Native navigation and PTY resource loading deferred until the terminal tab becomes visible.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
What's new in version 1.22.0:

  • Color & Theme Customization: Added settings interface with preset themes (Dracula, Solarized Dark/Light, Monokai, One Dark, Konsole Default), fully custom scheme creation, and a compact 2x8 grid picker for the 16 ANSI colors.
  • Background Opacity (Konsole Style): Added a background transparency slider (0-100%) that dynamically adjusts the terminal background opacity over the IDE tool window background while keeping text, selections, and cursors fully opaque and readable.
  • Shift + Enter Shortcut: Map Shift + Enter to backslash + enter (\\\r) to facilitate multiline text editing and newlines in CLI chat agents (like agy) and shell line continuations.

What's new in version 1.20.0:

  • Configurable Scrollbar Visibility: Added settings option to show or hide the vertical scrollbar, aligning with different layout preferences.
  • Mac Scroll/Rubber-Banding Fix: Fixed trackpad overscroll and rubber-banding flickering in macOS/JBR by introducing a locked viewport position.
  • Clean Telemetry HUD: Disabled debug/diagnostic telemetry overlay HUD by default for clean production builds.
  • Deprecation Warnings Resolve: Fixed OLD_EDT thread deprecation warnings on tab actions to ensure compatibility with modern IntelliJ platform versions.
  • English Documentation: Fully translated all user-facing plugin descriptions and release change-notes to English.

What's new in version 1.19:

  • Bracketed Paste Mode Support (ANSI 2004): Implemented full support for ANSI mode `2004`. When pasting long or multiline text, the emulator now wraps the block with special escape sequences (`\u001b[200~` and `\u001b[201~`). This prevents the shell (like `zsh`/`bash`) from interpreting the block character-by-character too quickly, avoiding cursor/redraw desynchronization and resolving visual corruption and misalignment on long pasted lines.

What's new in version 1.18:

  • IDE Global Shortcut Interception (e.g., Ctrl + R): Implemented a high-priority event dispatcher (`IdeEventQueue.EventDispatcher`) that captures and consumes global IDE keyboard shortcuts when the terminal emulator has focus. This prevents PyCharm from intercepting crucial shell combinations (like `Ctrl+R` for history search, `Ctrl+C`, `Ctrl+Z`, etc.). Essential shortcuts like toggling ToolWindows (`Cmd`/`Alt` + `1..9`) or modifiers alone are excluded to keep `Double Shift` functioning.

What's new in version 1.17:

  • Command Autocompletion with Tab Key Support: Disabled Swing's default focus traversal keys interception for the terminal panel. This allows `Tab` and `Shift + Tab` keystrokes to be delivered directly to the terminal emulator instead of switching UI components in IntelliJ. Thus, native shell autocompletion (`zsh`/`bash`) and tab-dependent commands now work transparently and directly.

What's new in version 1.16:

  • Orphaned Characters Fix when Exiting Interactive Programs (Letter 'q' bug): Added support for intermediate characters (range `0x20..0x2F`, like space) within CSI escape sequences. This fixes a bug where cursor style change commands (`DECSCUSR` sequence: `CSI Ps SP q`) broke halfway due to the intermediate space character, causing the trailing `q` to print as ordinary text when exiting `/help` or other interactive console utilities.

What's new in version 1.15:

  • Cursor Save and Restore Support (Save/Restore Cursor): Added full support for ANSI cursor save and restore sequences (`ESC 7`/`ESC 8` and CSI `s`/`CSI u`). This fixes prompt misalignment, offset issues, and unwanted space insertions when navigating word-by-word (`opt + arrows` on macOS) or refreshing complex prompts in Zsh (e.g., with syntax-highlighting or autosuggestions plugins).

What's new in version 1.14:

  • Scrollback Margins Support (DECSTBM): Implemented full support for CSI `r` sequence to define top and bottom scrolling margins. This fixes bugs in interactive carousel-like menus (where items rotate/slide in a screen sub-region while the rest remains fixed), respecting margins during line insertions and deletions (`IL`, `DL`) and line wraps.

What's new in version 1.13:

  • Terminal Font Proportion and Size Fix: Bound automatic typography to PyCharm's specific console settings (`consoleFontName` and `consoleFontSize`), rather than editor font settings.
  • Compact and Precise Line Height: Implemented a default line height of `1.0` for the terminal. This prevents excessive vertical spacing, letting the correct number of rows fit so interactive menus are not clipped and box-drawing characters join vertically without gaps.

What's new in version 1.12:

  • Vertical Resize Duplication Fix: Implemented an adaptive resizing algorithm anchored to the bottom that preserves cursor position in the history buffer. This prevents duplication of interactive menus, banners, and prompts (like `agy` and `inquirer`) when dragging to change terminal height.

What's new in version 1.11:

  • Advanced Scroll and Line ANSI Support: Added support for `ESC M` (Reverse Index) and CSI `E` (Cursor Next Line), `F` (Cursor Previous Line), `S` (Scroll Up), and `T` (Scroll Down) commands. This fixes line duplication issues in dynamic menus and interactive prompts (like `inquirer` and similar).
  • Advanced Text Deletion and Navigation Shortcuts: Added system shortcuts for text deletion and navigation:
    • macOS: `Opt+Backspace` (delete previous word), `Cmd+Backspace` (delete entire line), `Opt+Arrows` (move word-by-word), `Cmd+Arrows` (move to start/end of line).
    • Windows/Linux: `Ctrl+Backspace` (delete previous word), `Alt+Backspace` (delete entire line), `Ctrl+Arrows` (move word-by-word), `Alt+Arrows` (move to start/end of line).
  • Modifier Key Combinations: Added full support for modifier key combinations (Shift, Alt, Ctrl) combined with navigation and editing keys (arrows, Home, End, PageUp, PageDown, Delete) in accordance with the standard xterm emulator (e.g. `Ctrl+Home` and `Ctrl+End`).
  • Advanced ANSI Support: Added support for CSI DCH, ECH, ICH, IL, and DL commands. This fixes text overlapping and corruption in interactive tools like `agy` file lists and terminal editors.
  • Pure Swing Engine: Complete migration from JCEF to native Swing, removing the embedded Chromium engine.
  • Lazy Loading: System processes are launched only when the Tool Window becomes visible.
  • Autodetect Virtual Environment: Automatic activation of Python virtual environments on shell startup.
  • History Limit: Option added in settings to adjust lines in the scrollback buffer.
  • Login Shells: Forced execution as a Login Shell on Unix/macOS to inherit system environment variables (PATH, aliases, etc.).
  • Keyboard Input Fixes: Separated PTY read and write threads to prevent keyboard lockups. Removed Enter character duplication in `keyTyped`.
  • Sizing Safeguard: Prevents shell lockups by avoiding sending microscopic window sizes (1x1) during startup.
